news.t: Why Japan's $70 billion-plus intervention and a rate hike didn't prop up the yen more
Japan's Consumer Inflation Held Steady in May
Japan core inflation holds steady in May, matching expectations despite energy price concerns
Bank of Japan Could Raise Rates Even If Mideast Uncertainty Persists
BOJ should signal clear rate path after June hike, SMFG markets chief says
Japanese bond yields are the highest in 40 years. The budget and a 'red flag' from PM Takaichi have markets nervous
Foreign investors buy Japanese stocks for eighth week on AI rally
Nikkei Rises 1.3%, Led by Chip, Metals Stocks
Japan Consumer Inflation Slows Despite Middle East Effects
Japan inflation softens more than expected in April, weakening case for BOJ rate hike

holdings.infoBox: FLAX is an equity ETF with a total of 1611 individual holdings. The top holdings are Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company stock at 14.67%, Samsung Electronics at 8.50%, SK hynix at 6.77%, Tencent Holdings at 2.81%, and Alibaba Group Holding at 1.94%.

overview.description: The Franklin FTSE Asia ex Japan ETF (FLAX) is an exchange-traded fund that is based on the FTSE Asia ex Japan RIC Capped index. The fund tracks a market cap-weighted index of large- and mid-cap stocks from developed and emerging Asian countries, excluding Japan. FLAX was launched on Feb 6, 2018 and is issued by Franklin Templeton.

data.text: Consumer inflation remained steady last month, with economists noting that the full effect of higher oil prices has yet to emerge.

data.title: Bank of Japan Could Raise Rates Even If Mideast Uncertainty Persists
data.text: Japan's central bank could look past Middle East uncertainty and raise interest rates if inflation becomes a bigger threat to the economy than the risk of slowing growth, Gov. Kazuo Ueda said.

data.title: Foreign investors buy Japanese stocks for eighth week on AI rally
data.text: Foreign investors bought Japanese stocks for the eighth straight week through May 23, as oil prices eased and ​AI-related shares rallied on robust demand outlook.

data.title: Nikkei Rises 1.3%, Led by Chip, Metals Stocks
data.text: Japanese stocks were higher in early trade thanks to continued hopes for U.S.-Iran peace talks.

data.title: Japan Consumer Inflation Slows Despite Middle East Effects
data.text: Japan's consumer inflation cooled in April as government measures capped price growth despite rising energy costs.

data.title: Japan inflation softens more than expected in April, weakening case for BOJ rate hike
data.text: Energy costs remained in focus as lawmakers pushed subsidy measures. Strong exports supported growth and kept rate hike expectations alive.

data.title: Bank of Japan Policymaker Signals That Rate Hike Might Be Approaching
data.text: Policy board member Junko Koeda said Japan's underlying inflation is likely already at around 2%.

data.title: Japan may have fired its yen bazooka twice, but markets are testing Tokyo's resolve
data.text: On Wednesday, the yen appreciated sharply, fueling speculation that Tokyo had intervened a second time. Analysts said interest rate gaps continued driving weakness in the yen.

data.title: Japan's Exports Fall for Fourth Straight Month Under Weight of Tariffs
data.text: Japan's exports fell 0.1% in August, declining for a fourth consecutive month as higher U.S. tariffs crimped outbound trade.

data.title: Japan's exports down in August as automakers grapple with US tariffs
